item.title: 🇺🇦 US approves $108 million HAWK missile system sale to Ukraine
item.description: The US State Department approved a $108 million sale of HAWK missile system equipment to Ukraine, including maintenance support, spare parts, and logistics assistance for the air defense systems.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇭🇺 Ukraine foreign minister meets new Hungarian counterpart on minority rights
item.description: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha held his first meeting with Hungary's new Foreign Minister Anita Orban in Sweden to discuss minority rights and bilateral relations between the two countries.

item.title: 🕋 Hajj pilgrimage overshadowed by Middle East war tensions
item.description: Over a million Muslims gathered in Mecca for the hajj pilgrimage under the shadow of the ongoing Middle East conflict, with Saudi officials attempting to keep the war from visitors' minds following Iranian attacks on Gulf targets.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Refinery 700 km from Border
item.description: President Zelenskiy announced Ukrainian forces attacked a Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl, marking another long-range strike on Russian energy infrastructure as the war continues.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey Political Crisis Deepens as Opposition Vows Resistance
item.description: Turkeys opposition vowed to resist a court ruling that ousted its leader and annulled its congress, a move critics say aims to prolong President Erdogans 23-year rule.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Rise as Investors Doubt US-Iran Breakthrough
item.description: Oil prices climbed as investors remained skeptical about prospects for a breakthrough in US-Iran peace talks, with Brent crude holding elevated amid Hormuz shipping disruptions.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Cuts Oil Imports as Higher US Exports Wrongfoot Market
item.description: Chinese oil imports have fallen while US exports increased, catching market bulls off guard as physical crude prices hit all-time highs above $160 per barrel amid the Iran crisis.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Euro Zone Growth Set to Slow in 2026 as Middle East War Fuels Inflation
item.description: The European Commission warned that euro zone growth will slow in 2026 as the Middle East conflict triggers the second energy shock in less than five years.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Kremlin Denies China Trained Russian Soldiers for Ukraine War
item.description: The Kremlin dismissed as false a Reuters report that Chinas army secretly trained about 200 Russian soldiers in China late last year, some of whom went on to fight in Ukraine. The denial comes amid scrutiny of China-Russia military cooperation.

item.title: 🛢️ Russian Refinery Partially Shuts After Ukrainian Drone Strike
item.description: NORSI, Russias fourth-largest oil refinery, shut its main primary processing unit after a Ukrainian drone attack. The incident adds to a series of strikes on Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: ☢️ Trump Vows US Will Retrieve Irans Enriched Uranium
item.description: President Trump declared the United States will eventually recover Irans stockpile of highly enriched uranium, despite Tehran refusing to hand over the material. The statement underscores continued tensions over nuclear negotiations.

item.title: 🇻🇪 Exxon in Talks to Return to Venezuela After Two-Decade Absence
item.description: Exxon Mobil is negotiating to acquire oil production rights in Venezuela, nearly 20 years after being expelled from the country. The potential deal reflects shifting dynamics in global energy markets.

item.title: 🇮🇪 Ireland to Introduce Bill Curbing Trade With Israeli Settlements
item.description: Irelands foreign minister announced a bill to restrict trade with Israeli settlements in the occupied West Bank will be introduced to parliament in coming weeks. The move follows growing international pressure over settlement expansion.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇺🇸 Taiwan Denies Knowledge of US Arms Pause Despite Navy Secretary Statement
item.description: Taiwan presidential office says it has not received any notification of changes to military sales after Acting Navy Secretary Hung Cao said the US was pausing a $14 billion arms package to ensure munitions for the Iran operation.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇦🇪 Netanyahu Leaked Secret UAE Wartime Visit to Boost Domestic Standing
item.description: Analysis reveals Israeli PM Netanyahu deliberately disclosed his clandestine March meeting with UAE President MBZ to portray himself as a regional statesman ahead of elections, drawing Emirati anger and potentially undermining Gulf balancing act.

item.title: 🇵🇸🏥 Injured Palestinian Girls Arrive in Boston for Medical Treatment
item.description: Three Palestinian children from Gaza, including a double amputee and burn victims, arrived in the US for urgent medical care as part of HEAL Palestine evacuation efforts, highlighting Gaza healthcare collapse.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇭🇰 UK Refuses to Revoke Hong Kong Trade Office Privileges After Spy Case
item.description: British government says Hong Kong Economic and Trade Office can continue legitimate activity despite conviction of two men linked to the office for spying on activists, resisting political pressure to revoke diplomatic privileges.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇮🇷 Pakistan mediates US-Iran peace talks as uranium dispute persists
item.description: Iran's foreign minister met his Pakistani counterpart to discuss proposals to end the US-Israeli war. Tehran and Washington remain at odds over uranium stockpile and Strait of Hormuz controls.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇯🇵🇦🇺🇮🇳 Quad foreign ministers to meet in Delhi amid China tensions
item.description: The US, Japan, Australia and India will hold a meeting of Quad foreign ministers in New Delhi on May 26, Japan's foreign ministry announced.

item.title: 🇹🇷⚖️ Turkish court ousts main opposition leader Ozel
item.description: A Turkish court annulled the 2023 party congress that elected opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozel, effectively removing him from his position and dealing a blow to President Erdogan's challengers.

item.title: 🇨🇳🛢️ China's Hengli petrochemical empire faces US sanctions chill
item.description: The Hengli Group, one of China's largest private oil refiners, has been thrust into the center of a geopolitical power struggle as US sanctions target the company.

item.title: 🌏🤝 APEC trade envoys gather in China to discuss supply chain resilience
item.description: Asia-Pacific trade envoys met in China to discuss multilateral cooperation and supply chain resilience amid global shocks including the US-Iranian conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇱 Trump announces additional 5,000 US troops for Poland
item.description: President Trump declared the US will send 5,000 additional troops to Poland, reversing recent administration moves to reduce European deployments. The announcement stirred confusion amid contradictory statements about US troop presence in Europe.

item.title: 🇧🇾🇺🇦 Belarus will not be dragged into Ukraine war, Lukashenko says
item.description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko dismissed any notion Belarus would join Russia in the Ukraine war, but stated Belarus and Russia would jointly defend themselves if attacked. The statement comes amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: 🇱🇻🇱🇹 Latvia and Lithuania issue drone alerts, NATO jets scramble
item.description: Lithuania and Latvia detected drones in their airspace and urged residents to seek shelter while NATO fighter jets scrambled to intercept. This marks the latest in a series of security incidents in the Baltic states.
